questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText:
Which one of the following statements regarding simple pendulum is correct?

Simple pendulum has a time period independent of amplitude:
options: only for small amplitudes because then the net force on its bob is independent of its displacement.
for any amplitude because the net force on the bob is always proportional to its displacement.
for any amplitude because the net force on the bob is independent of its displacement.
only for small amplitudes because then the net force on its bob is proportional to its displacement.
questions.solution:
In order to accurately describe the behavior of a simple pendulum, we need to analyze its physics in detail. A simple pendulum consists of a mass (known as the bob) attached to a string of length $ L $ and fixed at a pivot point. When displaced from its equilibrium position and released, it oscillates back and forth under the influence of gravity.

The restoring force acting on the bob is provided by the component of the gravitational force that acts along the direction of the arc. This force can be expressed as:

$$ F = -mg \sin(\theta) $$

where:

  • F is the restoring force,
  • m is the mass of the bob,
  • g is the acceleration due to gravity,
  • θ is the angular displacement from the vertical.

For small angular displacements (small amplitudes), $ \sin(\theta) $ can be approximated by $ \theta $ (when $ \theta $ is measured in radians). Thus, the restoring force becomes:

$$ F \approx -mg\theta $$

Since $ \theta = \frac{x}{L} $ where $ x $ is the linear displacement and $ L $ is the length of the pendulum, the force can be rewritten as:

$$ F \approx -\frac{mg}{L} x $$

This shows that the restoring force is directly proportional to the displacement, characteristic of simple harmonic motion. The time period $ T $ of a simple pendulum is then given by:

$$ T = 2\pi \sqrt{\frac{L}{g}} $$

Note that this time period is independent of amplitude, but the approximation $ \sin(\theta) \approx \theta $ only holds for small amplitudes. Therefore, the correct statement regarding the simple pendulum is:

Option D: only for small amplitudes because then the net force on its bob is proportional to its displacement.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: Which one of the following statements is correct ?
options: A neutron is formed by combination of an electron and a proton. Therefore, it is neutral
The mass of an electron is about 1/2000 times that of a proton
An isotope of cobalt is used in the treatment of goiter
J. J. Thomson proposed that the nucleus of an atom contains only neutrons
questions.solution:
Option 2 is correct.

Explanation:

The mass of an electron is

$$m_e \approx 9.11\times10^{-31}\,\text{kg},$$

while that of a proton is

$$m_p \approx 1.67\times10^{-27}\,\text{kg}.$$

Their ratio is

$$\frac{m_e}{m_p}\approx\frac{1}{1836}\approx\frac{1}{2000}.$$

Why the others are wrong:

Option 1 describes an outdated idea; neutrons aren’t simply proton+electron combinations.

Option 3 mixes up treatments: radioiodine isotopes help treat goiter, not cobalt.

Option 4 is incorrect—J. J. Thomson’s model didn’t place neutrons in the nucleus (neutrons were discovered later by Chadwick).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: The streaming of light beams coming from the Sun through trees is said to have suggested that light travels in straight line. The particles on the path of light beams are visible to us because
options: dust particles in the air reflect light into our eyes
dust particles in the air scatter light into our eyes
dust particles in the air refract light into our eyes
dust particles in the air polarize light into our eyes
questions.solution:
The phenomenon you are describing is commonly observed, especially when sunlight streams through forest canopies or through windows into a dusty room. This effect provides evidence that light travels in straight lines and also makes the particles in the light path visible to the observer.

Among the provided options:

Option A: suggests that dust particles in the air reflect light into our eyes. While reflection does involve bouncing light off surfaces, in this context, it is not the primary reason why we see the particles.

Option B: suggests that dust particles in the air scatter light into our eyes. This is the correct explanation. Scattering occurs when light is deflected in many different directions by small particles. When sunlight strikes dust particles, the light is scattered in various directions, making the dust particles visible from multiple angles. This scattered light enters our eyes, allowing us to see the particles.

Option C: suggests that dust particles in the air refract light into our eyes. Refraction refers to the bending of light as it passes through different media, which is not the primary mechanism here.

Option D: suggests that dust particles in the air polarize light into our eyes. Polarization refers to the orientation of light waves in certain directions, which is also not the primary reason for visibility in this scenario.

Therefore, the correct answer is:

Option B: dust particles in the air scatter light into our eyes.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: Which one of the following wavelength radiations may correspond to an X-ray ?
questions.solution:
X-rays have very short wavelengths, usually in the range of about

$$ 0.01 \text{ nm to } 10 \text{ nm} $$

Now check the options:

  • $1 \text{ nm}$: lies in the X-ray range

  • $10 \text{ nm}$: at the extreme end, but usually not taken as the standard answer here

  • $100 \text{ nm}$: ultraviolet region

  • $300 \text{ nm}$: ultraviolet region

So, the correct answer is:

$$ \boxed{1 \text{ nm}} $$

Option 1.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: Which one among the following diagrams may correctly represent the motion of a skydiver during a jump ?
questions.solution:
CONCEPT:

How a Skydiver Falls

Step 1: Skydiver Jumps

When a skydiver jumps out, gravity pulls them down. Their speed goes up fast at first because they are falling freely through the air.

Step 2: Reaching Terminal Velocity

As the skydiver falls faster, air pushes back harder. Eventually, the push from the air (air resistance) matches the pull of gravity, so the skydiver's speed stops increasing. This steady speed is called terminal velocity.

Step 3: Opening the Parachute

When the parachute opens, there is a sudden big increase in air resistance. This makes the skydiver slow down very quickly.

Speed-Time Graph:

The speed-time graph starts by going up quickly (as the skydiver speeds up). Then, it flattens out (terminal velocity). After the parachute opens, the line drops sharply as the skydiver slows down fast.

EXPLANATION:

Option 1:

This graph is correct. It shows speed going up fast, staying the same for a while (terminal velocity), and then dropping quickly after the parachute opens.

Option 2:

This option is incorrect because it makes the speed go to zero too fast, which doesn't happen in real skydiving.

Option 3:

This option is wrong because it shows speed going up and down in a straight line, as if the skydiver stops suddenly without a parachute. This is unrealistic.

Option 4:

This option almost matches what really happens, but it does not clearly show a flat part for terminal velocity and the slowing down isn't sudden enough when the parachute opens.

So, Option 1 is correct.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText:
Which of the following statements give characteristics of contact forces?

1. It appears between an object when it is in contact with some other object

2. It satisfies the third law of motion

3. It may appear between a pair of solid and fluid

Select the answer using the code given below:
questions.solution:
1. Definition of contact force

    – A force that arises only when two bodies are in physical contact.

  2. Check statement (1): “It appears between an object when it is in contact with some other object”

    – This is exactly the definition of a contact force.

    ⇒ Statement 1 is correct.

  3. Check statement (2): “It satisfies the third law of motion”

    – Newton’s third law, “To every action there is an equal and opposite reaction,” holds for all forces, including contact forces.

    ⇒ Statement 2 is correct.

  4. Check statement (3): “It may appear between a pair of solid and fluid”

    – Examples:

    • Fluid pressure on a submerged plate (normal contact force)

    • Viscous drag between a fluid and a moving solid

    ⇒ Statement 3 is correct.

All three statements are true.

Answer: Option 4 (1, 2 and 3)

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: The commercial unit of electrical energy is kilowatt-hour (kWh), which is equal to

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: We hear an echo due to
questions.solution:
Concept:

Echo: An echo occurs when a sound wave reflects off a surface and is heard a short time after the original sound. This delay is caused by the time it takes for the sound wave to travel to the surface and back.

Reflection of sound: When sound waves encounter a hard surface, they bounce back, producing a reflected sound wave.

Conditions for Echo Formation:

The distance between the sound source and the reflecting surface should be at least 17.2 meters, assuming the speed of sound is approximately 344 m/s.

The reflecting surface should be large and hard to effectively reflect the sound.

Therefore, the phenomenon of hearing an echo is explained by the reflection of sound waves.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: A DC generator works on the principle of
questions.solution:
A DC generator works on the principle of:

Option C

Faraday’s laws of electromagnetic induction

Explanation:

Faraday's laws of electromagnetic induction are fundamental principles that describe how electric currents can be induced in conductors due to changing magnetic fields. A DC generator converts mechanical energy into electrical energy using these principles. When a conductor (such as a coil of wire) moves through a magnetic field, it cuts through the field lines, resulting in an induced electromotive force (EMF) or voltage in the conductor. This phenomenon is described by Faraday's laws, which state:

  • First Law: Any change in the magnetic field within a closed loop of wire will induce an EMF in the wire.
  • Second Law: The magnitude of the induced EMF is directly proportional to the rate of change of the magnetic flux through the loop.

Therefore, a DC generator operates based on these laws to produce a continuous direct current (DC) output.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: A bullet of mass 10 g is horizontally fired with velocity 300 m s<sup>-1</sup> from a pistol of mass 1 kg. What is the recoil velocity of the pistol?
questions.solution:
To determine the recoil velocity of the pistol, we can use the principle of conservation of momentum. According to this principle, the total momentum before and after firing the bullet must be the same because there are no external forces acting on the system.

Given:

  • Mass of the bullet, $ m_b = 10 \, \text{g} = 0.01 \, \text{kg} $
  • Velocity of the bullet, $ v_b = 300 \, \text{m/s} $
  • Mass of the pistol, $ m_p = 1 \, \text{kg} $
  • Recoil velocity of the pistol, $ v_p = ? $

The total momentum before firing is zero because both the bullet and the pistol are at rest initially. Therefore, the total momentum after firing should also be zero.

Let us denote the recoil velocity of the pistol as $ v_p $. The momentum of the bullet and the pistol after firing are given by:

$$ \begin{align*} \text{Momentum of the bullet} & = m_b \cdot v_b \\ \text{Momentum of the pistol} & = m_p \cdot v_p \end{align*} $$

According to the conservation of momentum:

$$ m_b \cdot v_b + m_p \cdot v_p = 0 $$

Solving for $ v_p $:

$$ v_p = -\frac{m_b \cdot v_b}{m_p} $$

Substituting the given values:

$$ \begin{align*} v_p &= -\frac{0.01 \, \text{kg} \times 300 \, \text{m/s}}{1 \, \text{kg}} \\ v_p &= -3 \, \text{m/s} \end{align*} $$

Thus, the recoil velocity of the pistol is $ -3 \, \text{m/s} $. The negative sign indicates that the pistol moves in the opposite direction to that of the bullet.

Hence, the correct option is:

Option C: -3 m s-1

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: Which one of the following is dimensionless quantity?
questions.solution:
Strain is the only dimensionless quantity among the four.

• Stress has units of force per area:

$$[{\rm Stress}] = \frac{F}{A} = \frac{MLT^{-2}}{L^2} = M\,L^{-1}\,T^{-2}.$$

• Pressure is the same dimensionally as stress:

$$[{\rm Pressure}] = M\,L^{-1}\,T^{-2}.$$

• Force has units:

$$[F] = M\,L\,T^{-2}.$$

• Strain is a ratio of lengths:

$$\text{Strain} = \frac{\Delta L}{L}$$

so all the length dimensions cancel, making it dimensionless.

Answer: Option 2 (Strain).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: Shown in the figure are two plane mirrors XY and YZ (XY ⊥ YZ) joined at their edge. Also shown is a light ray falling on one of the mirrors and reflected back parallel to its original path as a result of this arrangement. The two mirrors are now rotated by an angle θ to their new position X'YZ', as shown. As a result the new reflected ray is at an angle α from the original reflected ray. Then :
questions.solution:
Explanation:

When two plane mirrors are at right angles (90°), a light ray falling on one mirror reflects back in exactly the opposite direction.

This makes the angle between the incoming ray and the outgoing ray equal to 180°.

In the figure, mirrors XY and YZ meet at a right angle.

Now the pair of mirrors is rotated by an angle θ to the new position X'YZ'.

After this rotation, the reflected ray shifts by an angle α compared to the original reflected ray.

However, the reflected ray remains parallel to its original direction, so α = 0°.

Therefore, the new reflected ray is parallel to the original reflected ray.

Additional Information

If two mirrors are placed at an angle other than 90°, the incoming ray and the reflected ray are no longer exactly opposite.

In that case, the angles of the reflected rays depend on the angle between the mirrors and on the incidence and reflection angles at each mirror.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: Which one among the following statements is correct ?
options: Pressure has magnitude as well as direction:
As we go deeper in a liquid, the pressure decreases
Action and reaction forces act on the same object
Pascal and N/m<sup>2</sup> represent the same unit
questions.solution:
The correct statement is:

$$\text{Option 4: Pascal and } N/m^2 \text{ represent the same unit}$$

Now let us check each option one by one:

  1. Pressure has magnitude as well as direction

    This is wrong because pressure is a scalar quantity.

    It has only magnitude, not direction.

  2. As we go deeper in a liquid, the pressure decreases

    This is wrong.

    In a liquid, pressure increases with depth.

  3. Action and reaction forces act on the same object

    This is wrong.

    According to Newton’s third law, action and reaction act on different objects.

  4. Pascal and $N/m^2$ represent the same unit

    This is correct because

$$1 \, \text{pascal} = 1 \, N/m^2$$

So, the correct answer is Option 4.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: When a light ray passes through from air to water with a non-zero angle the ray will be
questions.solution:
When light goes from air (n₁≈1.00) into water (n₂≈1.33) at some angle θ₁ to the normal, Snell’s law tells us:

$$n_1\sin\theta_1 \;=\; n_2\sin\theta_2\,. $$

Because n₂>n₁, we have sin θ₂<sin θ₁ ⇒ θ₂<θ₁.

That means the refracted ray bends toward the normal.

Answer: Option 1 – bending towards the normal.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: A wooden box of mass 2 kg and dimensions (30 cm × 15 cm × 10 cm) is placed on a table with sides 30 cm and 10 cm touching the tabletop. Which one of the following is the approximate pressure exerted on the table?
options: 111.1 N/m2
222.2 N/m2
333.3 N/m2
666.6 N/m2

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText:
Which of the following forces is/are fundamental in nature?

1. Gravitational force

2. Electromagnetic forces

3. Strong and weak nuclear forces

Select the correct answer using the code given below :
questions.solution:
In the realm of physics, fundamental forces, also known as fundamental interactions, are the basic forces that govern the interactions between particles and objects. There are four recognized fundamental forces in nature :

1. Gravitational Force: This is the force of attraction that acts between all masses. Despite being the weakest of the four forces, it has an infinite range and is always attractive. It's described by Newton's law of gravitation and Einstein's general theory of relativity.

2. Electromagnetic Forces: These forces act between charged particles and are described by Coulomb's law and Maxwell's equations. They have an infinite range and include electric forces (between stationary charges) and magnetic forces (between moving charges).

3. Strong Nuclear Force: This is the force that holds the protons and neutrons together in an atom's nucleus. Despite its extremely short range (approximately the size of an atomic nucleus, $$10^{-15}$$ meters), it is the strongest of the four fundamental forces.

4. Weak Nuclear Force: This force is responsible for certain types of particle decay processes, such as beta decay in radioactive atoms. Its range is even smaller than that of the strong nuclear force, approximately $$10^{-18}$$ meters, and it is weaker than both the strong nuclear and the electromagnetic forces.

Given this information, the correct answer is :

Option D : 1, 2 and 3

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: Which one of the following statements about earthquake waves is not correct?
options: P waves move faster and are the first to arrive at the surface.
P waves can travel through gaseous, liquid and solid materials.
Seismographs located beyond 145° from epicentre can record the arrival of P waves.
P waves have maximum area covered under its shadow zone.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: The heating element in an electric iron is usually made of
questions.solution:
The heating element in an electric iron is typically made of a material that has a high resistance to generate sufficient heat when electric current passes through it. Among the options provided, the best choice for this application is Nichrome, which is a nickel-chromium alloy. Nichrome is preferred because it has a high melting point, excellent resistance to oxidation, and stable resistance over a wide range of temperatures.

Therefore, the correct answer is:

Option C Nichrome

To elaborate more, when electric current flows through Nichrome, it encounters resistance, converting electrical energy into heat energy. This principle of Joule heating is utilized in various heating applications, such as in electric irons, toasters, and hair dryers. The equation governing this process is given by:

$$Q = I^2 R t$$

where:

  • $$Q$$ is the heat energy produced in joules
  • $$I$$ is the current in amperes
  • $$R$$ is the resistance in ohms
  • $$t$$ is the time in seconds

Using Nichrome allows manufacturers to create reliable and efficient heating elements that can withstand high temperatures and prolonged usage.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: A stone is thrown horizontally from the top of a 20 m high building with a speed of 12 m/s. It hits the ground at a distance R from the building. Taking g = 10 m/s<sup>2</sup> and neglecting air resistance will give :
questions.solution:
1. Vertical motion

  • Initial vertical velocity $u_y = 0$

  • Height $h = 20\,$m

  • Acceleration $g = 10\,$m/s²

  • Use $$h = \tfrac12\,g\,t^2$$

    so $$20 = \tfrac12\cdot10\cdot t^2 \quad\Longrightarrow\quad t^2 = 4 \quad\Longrightarrow\quad t = 2\ \text{s}$$

  1. Horizontal motion

  • Horizontal speed $v_x = 12\,$m/s (constant)

  • Range $R = v_x \,t = 12 \times 2 = 24\,$m

Answer: R = 24 m (Option C)

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: In the dispersion of white light by a common glass prism, which one among the following is correct?
options: Red light deviates the most because red light has highest speed in prism
Blue light deviates the most because blue light has highest speed in prism
Red light deviates the most because red light has lowest speed in prism
Blue light deviates the most because blue light has lowest speed in prism
questions.solution:
In the dispersion of white light by a common glass prism, the different colors of light have different speeds when traveling through the prism. The speed of light in a medium is inversely related to the refractive index of that medium for that particular color. As a result, light with a shorter wavelength (like blue light) slows down more and, therefore, bends more than light with a longer wavelength (like red light) when it enters and exits the prism.

When white light passes through a prism, it is dispersed into its constituent colors due to the difference in the refractive indices for different wavelengths. Blue light has a shorter wavelength compared to red light, which means it has a higher refractive index in the glass prism. Consequently, blue light deviates or bends more than red light.

So, the correct answer is:

Option D: Blue light deviates the most because blue light has the lowest speed in the prism.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: An object is made of two equal parts by volume; one part has density ρ<sub>0</sub>, and the other part has density 2ρ<sub>0</sub>. What is the average density of the object?

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: What is the nature of velocity-time graph for a car moving with uniform acceleration?
questions.solution:
The nature of the velocity-time graph for a car moving with uniform acceleration is a straight line. This is because uniform acceleration means that the velocity of the car changes at a constant rate over time.

Mathematically, if a car is moving with uniform acceleration, the relationship between its velocity $ v $ and time $ t $ can be described by the equation:

$$ v = u + at $$

Where:

  • $ v $ is the final velocity.
  • $ u $ is the initial velocity.
  • $ a $ is the constant acceleration.
  • $ t $ is the time.

This equation is of the form of a linear equation $ y = mx + c $, where $ m $ (the slope) represents the acceleration $ a $, and $ c $ (the y-intercept) represents the initial velocity $ u $. Therefore, the graph of velocity versus time will be a straight line.

Thus, the correct answer is:

Option C: Straight line

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: Mirage is an illustration of
options: only total internal reflection of light.
both refraction and total internal reflection of light.
questions.solution:
A mirage is an illustration of both refraction and total internal reflection of light.

Explanation:

Mirages occur when layers of air at different temperatures cause light to bend. This is primarily due to refraction, which is the bending of light as it passes from one medium to another with a different density.

In the case of a hot road, the air near the surface is significantly warmer than the air above it. Light from the sky bends as it moves through these layers of varying density, and in some cases, this bending can continue until the angle of incidence exceeds the critical angle for total internal reflection.

Key Concepts:

Refraction: Occurs when light waves pass from one medium to another and change speed, causing the light to bend. This bending creates the initial path alteration of light.

Total Internal Reflection: After sufficient bending, if light strikes the boundary at a specific angle (greater than the critical angle), it reflects entirely back into the medium instead of passing through.

Together, refraction and total internal reflection help explain why a mirage makes a distant object, like the sky, appear as though it is reflecting off the ground, creating the illusion of water. Hence, Option D - both refraction and total internal reflection of light - is correct.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: The frequency (f), wavelength (λ) and speed (v) of a sound wave are related as
options: f=vλ
λ=vf
$f = \frac{\lambda}{v}$​
v=λf
questions.solution:
The correct relation is

$$v = \lambda\,f$$

so the answer is Option 4.

(You can also write it in the equivalent forms

• $$f = \frac{v}{\lambda}$$

• $$\lambda = \frac{v}{f}\,$$.)

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: The energy possessed by a body due to its change in position or shape is called

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following materials is not an allotrope of carbon ?
questions.solution:
Option C: Fly ash is not an allotrope of carbon.

Allotropes of carbon are different structural forms of the same element, where the atoms are bonded together in different patterns or arrangements. Here, we can explore the given choices:

Diamond: In diamond, each carbon atom tetrahedrally coordinates with four other carbon atoms in a 3-dimensional network, making it an allotrope with a rigid structure.

Graphene: Graphene consists of a single layer of carbon atoms arranged in a two-dimensional honeycomb lattice, making it one of the known allotropes of carbon.

Fly ash: This is a byproduct from burning pulverized coal in electric power generating plants. It primarily consists of silicon dioxide (SiO₂), aluminum oxide (Al₂O₃), and iron oxide (Fe₂O₃) but doesn't have any specific arrangement of carbon atoms, hence is not considered an allotrope of carbon.

Fullerene: Fullerenes are molecules of carbon atoms arranged in a closed shell, typically in spherical, ellipsoidal, or cylindrical shapes, and are indeed allotropes of carbon.

In summary, amongst the options provided, fly ash does not qualify as an allotrope of carbon.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: The process whereby certain minerals absorb water, expand and change is called as
questions.solution:
The process whereby certain minerals absorb water, expand, and undergo a chemical change is called Hydration.

In geological contexts, hydration is often observed when minerals like anhydrite transform into gypsum. This process involves the incorporation of water molecules into the mineral structure, leading to an increase in volume and a change in the chemical composition. Hydration is distinct from other weathering processes like oxidation, hydrolysis, and carbonation, each of which involves different chemical reactions and agents.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one among the following is present in the nettle leaf hairs that causes burning pain?
questions.solution:
The substance present in nettle leaf hairs that causes a burning pain is Methanoic acid. Also known as formic acid, methanoic acid is responsible for the stinging sensation experienced when one comes into contact with nettle leaves.

So, the correct answer is:

Option A

Methanoic acid

Methanoic acid, or formic acid, has the molecular formula $$HCOOH$$. This compound is known for its ability to irritate and cause pain upon skin contact.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following is NOT a heterogeneous mixture ?
options: Mixture of sugar 8 and salt crystals
questions.solution:
The correct answer is:

$$\boxed{\text{Option 1: Sulphur in carbon disulphide}}$$

Step-by-step:

A heterogeneous mixture has non-uniform composition, and its different parts can be seen separately.

Now check each option:

  1. Sulphur in carbon disulphide

    Sulphur dissolves completely in carbon disulphide, so it forms a homogeneous mixture, not a heterogeneous mixture.

  2. Mixture of sugar and salt crystals

    Sugar crystals and salt crystals remain separate, so it is a heterogeneous mixture.

  3. Sandy water

    Sand does not dissolve in water, so it is a heterogeneous mixture.

  4. Sulphur in water

    Sulphur does not dissolve in water, so it is a heterogeneous mixture.

Therefore, the one which is not a heterogeneous mixture is:

$$\boxed{\text{Sulphur in carbon disulphide}}$$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following statements is NOT correct ?
options: Water of crystallization is the fixed number of water molecules present in one formula unit of a salt
The molecular formula of Plaster of Paris is CaSO<sub>4</sub>:2H<sub>2</sub>O
Washing soda is used for removing the permanent hardness of water
The atmosphere of Venus is made up of thick white and yellow clouds of sulphuric acid
questions.solution:
The incorrect statement is:

$$\text{Option 2}$$

Reason:

Plaster of Paris has the formula:

$$\text{CaSO}_4 \cdot \dfrac{1}{2}\text{H}_2\text{O}$$

It is also written as:

$$2\text{CaSO}_4 \cdot \text{H}_2\text{O}$$

But in Option 2, it is given as:

$$\text{CaSO}_4 : 2\text{H}_2\text{O}$$

This is wrong.

Checking the other options:

  1. Water of crystallization is the fixed number of water molecules present in one formula unit of a salt — correct.

  2. Plaster of Paris formula given is wrong — not correct.

  3. Washing soda is used for removing permanent hardness of water — correct.

  4. Venus has thick white and yellow clouds of sulphuric acid — correct.

So, the answer is:

$$\boxed{\text{Option 2}}$$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following is the correct order of the valencies of elements Ne, Si, N and Mg?

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: For Aluminium (Al) (atomic number: 13, mass number: 27), which one among the following statements is NOT correct?
options: Number of electrons present in Al is 13
Number of protons present in Al is 13
Number of neutrons present in Al is 14
questions.solution:
Number of electrons = atomic number = 13 ✔

Number of protons = atomic number = 13 ✔

Number of neutrons = mass number – atomic number = $$27 - 13 = 14$$ ✔

Valency of Al is 2 ✘

Aluminium is in Group 13, so it commonly loses three electrons to form Al³⁺. Its usual valency is 3, not 2.

Thus, Option 4 is NOT correct.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one among the following is commonly used as an ‘anti-skinning agent’ in paints ?
questions.solution:
1. What is an anti-skinning agent?

    Paints contain oils (like linseed oil) or alkyd resins that dry by reacting with oxygen in the air (oxidation and polymerization). When paint is stored in a can, this drying process can start on the surface, forming a tough film or 'skin'. An anti-skinning agent is a substance added to paint to prevent or delay this film formation on the surface during storage. These agents usually work by acting as antioxidants, inhibiting the unwanted oxidation reaction.

  2. Evaluating the options:

    • Option 1: Gelatin

      Gelatin is a protein derived from collagen. It is mainly used as a gelling agent, thickener, or stabilizer in food, pharmaceuticals, and cosmetics. It is not used as an anti-skinning agent in paints.

    • Option 2: N-methyl pyrrolidone (NMP)

      NMP is a powerful polar aprotic solvent. It is used in many chemical processes, as a paint stripper, or as a solvent in some paint formulations. It does not function as an anti-skinning agent.

    • Option 3: Pyridine

      Pyridine is a basic heterocyclic organic compound. It is commonly used as a solvent and a reagent in organic synthesis. It is not known to be used as an anti-skinning agent in paints.

    • Option 4: Polyhydroxy phenol

      Phenols and their derivatives, especially those with sterically hindered hydroxyl groups (like butylated hydroxytoluene, BHT, or other substituted phenols), are well-known antioxidants. Antioxidants prevent unwanted oxidation reactions by scavenging free radicals. In paints, the skinning process is due to the oxidative polymerization of the binder. Therefore, polyhydroxy phenols (compounds with multiple hydroxyl groups attached to a benzene ring, or generally phenolic compounds) can act as effective anti-skinning agents by inhibiting this oxidation. Common anti-skinning agents include oximes (e.g., methyl ethyl ketoxime) and phenolic antioxidants.

Based on this, polyhydroxy phenols are commonly used as anti-skinning agents due to their antioxidant properties.

The final answer is $\boxed{\text{Polyhydroxy phenol}}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: For an element with atomic number 35, which one of the following will be the correct number of electrons in its valence shell based on Bohr's model of an atom?

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following reactions is an example of decomposition reaction?
options: CH4 (g) + 2O2 (g) → CO2(g) + 2H2O(g)

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one among the following oxides has the highest melting point?
questions.solution:
The oxides presented in the options are sodium oxide (Na$_2$O), magnesium oxide (MgO), iron(III) oxide (Fe$_2$O$_3$), and copper(II) oxide (CuO). To determine which one has the highest melting point, we need to consider the nature of the chemical bonding and lattice structure of each oxide.

1. Na$_2$O (Sodium Oxide): This is an ionic compound formed between sodium and oxygen and has ionic bonds. The melting point of Na$_2$O is relatively high but not as high as some other oxides on the list.

2. MgO (Magnesium Oxide): This is also an ionic compound but with a stronger ionic bond compared to Na$_2$O due to the higher charge density of Mg$^{2+}$ as opposed to Na$^{+}$. MgO has a very high melting point due to the strong electrostatic forces of attraction between Mg$^{2+}$ and O$^{2-}$ ions.

3. Fe$_2$O$_3$ (Iron(III) Oxide): This is an oxide of iron where iron is in the +3 oxidation state. Fe$_2$O$_3$ is composed of both ionic and some covalent character. Its melting point is also high but typically not as high as that of MgO.

4. CuO (Copper(II) Oxide): Copper(II) oxide has a partially covalent and partially ionic bond structure. Its melting point is high but lower compared to that of MgO and Fe$_2$O$_3$.

Among the given options, MgO (Magnesium Oxide) has the highest melting point due to its strong ionic bonds between Mg$^{2+}$ and O$^{2-}$ ions. The strong electrostatic forces result in a very high melting point, which is significantly higher than that of the other listed oxides.

Therefore, the correct answer is Option B: MgO.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following salts is responsible for temporary hardness of water ?
questions.solution:
Temporary hardness of water is caused by the bicarbonates of calcium and magnesium.

So, the correct answer is:

Option 3: Bicarbonates of magnesium

Reason:

  • Temporary hardness is due to soluble bicarbonates.

  • Permanent hardness is due to chlorides and sulphates of calcium and magnesium.

Therefore, magnesium bicarbonate is responsible for temporary hardness of water.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Element X forms a chloride with the formula XCl₂, which is a solid with high melting point. X would most likely be in the same group of periodic table as
questions.solution:
XCl₂ being a high-melting solid tells us it’s an ionic compound with X in the +2 oxidation state. Metals that commonly form MCl₂ salts are the Group 2 (alkaline-earth) elements.

• Group 2 elements → Be, Mg, Ca, …

• From the options given, Mg is the only Group 2 metal.

Answer: Option 3 (Mg)

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: The mass of 0:5 mole of N<sub>2</sub> gas is
questions.solution:
1. Molar mass of one N atom (from NCERT periodic table):

    $M(\mathrm{N}) = 14\ \text{g·mol}^{-1}$

  2. Molar mass of N₂ molecule:

    $$M(\mathrm{N_2}) = 2 \times M(\mathrm{N}) = 2 \times 14 = 28\ \text{g·mol}^{-1}$$

  3. Mass of $0.5$ mol of N₂:

    $$m = n \times M = 0.5\ \text{mol} \times 28\ \text{g·mol}^{-1} = 14\ \text{g}$$

Answer: 14 g (Option 2)

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following is also known as Dry ice in its solid form?
questions.solution:
Dry ice is actually the solid form of a very common gas. The compound known as dry ice is carbon dioxide.

Its chemical formula is $$CO_2$$.

Looking at the options you gave:

Option 1: $$SiO_2$$ is Silicon dioxide, which is more commonly known as silica.

Option 2: $$CO_2$$ is Carbon dioxide.

Option 3: $$CaO$$ is Calcium oxide, or quicklime.

Option 4: $$MgO$$ is Magnesium oxide.

So, the one that is also known as dry ice in its solid form is $$CO_2$$, Carbon dioxide.

That means Option 2 is the correct one! Dry ice gets its name because it doesn't melt into a liquid; instead, it goes directly from a solid to a gas, a process called sublimation.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one among the following elements is polyatomic ?
questions.solution:
1. Polyatomic element: an element whose molecules consist of more than two atoms.

  2. Common elemental forms:

  • Sodium: metallic lattice (atoms not in discrete molecules)

  • Aluminium: metallic lattice

  • Chlorine: diatomic molecules ($Cl_2$)

  • Sulphur: typically octatomic molecules ($S_8$)

  1. Sulphur exists as $S_8$ rings, a molecule with eight atoms.

Answer: Option 2 (Sulphur) is polyatomic.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText:
Match List I with List II and select the answer using the code given below the Lists:
List I (Chemical Name)  List II (Chemical Formula)
A. Washing soda  1. CaOCl₂
B. Baking soda 2. NaHCO₃
C. Bleaching powder  3. Na₂CO₃·10H₂O
D. Gypsum 4. CaSO₄·2H₂O

Code:
questions.solution:
Here’s how the chemicals match up:

Washing soda (A) is sodium carbonate decahydrate:

$$\text{Na}_2\text{CO}_3\cdot10\text{H}_2\text{O}$$ → 3

Baking soda (B) is sodium bicarbonate:

$$\text{NaHCO}_3$$ → 2

Bleaching powder (C) is calcium oxychloride:

$$\text{CaOCl}_2$$ → 1

Gypsum (D) is calcium sulfate dihydrate:

$$\text{CaSO}_4\cdot2\text{H}_2\text{O}$$ → 4

So the correct code is Option 4:

(a)–3, (b)–2, (c)–1, (d)–4

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following acid is used by goldsmith for cleaning of gold and silver articles?
questions.solution:
The correct answer is Option B: Nitric acid. Here's why:

Goldsmiths use nitric acid to clean gold and silver articles because of its unique properties:

  • Reacts with impurities: Nitric acid is a strong oxidizing agent. It reacts with common impurities found in gold and silver, such as copper, silver sulfide, and other metals. This reaction forms soluble salts, which can be easily washed away, leaving the precious metals clean.
  • Does not react with gold (in its pure form): While nitric acid is a strong acid, it doesn't readily react with pure gold. This is why it's effective for cleaning gold without damaging it.
  • Formation of Aqua Regia: When nitric acid is mixed with hydrochloric acid in a specific ratio, it creates a highly corrosive mixture known as Aqua Regia. Aqua Regia can dissolve gold, but goldsmiths typically use nitric acid alone for cleaning purposes.

The other options are not suitable for cleaning gold and silver:

  • Sulphuric acid (Option A): While sulphuric acid is a strong acid, it can react with both gold and silver, potentially damaging the articles.
  • Hydrochloric acid (Option C): Hydrochloric acid is less effective at removing impurities compared to nitric acid. It also reacts with silver, making it unsuitable for cleaning silver articles.
  • Phosphoric acid (Option D): Phosphoric acid is a weak acid and generally not strong enough to dissolve the impurities found in gold and silver.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following type of medicines is used for treating indigestion ?
questions.solution:
The correct answer is:

Option 4: Antacid

Explanation:

Indigestion is usually caused by excess acid in the stomach. Antacids are medicines that help to neutralise the extra acid and give relief from indigestion.

Other options:

  • Analgesic: used to relieve pain

  • Antiseptic: used to prevent infection in wounds

  • Antibiotic: used to kill or stop the growth of bacteria

So, the medicine used for treating indigestion is Antacid.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following metals can be extracted using carbon as reducing agent?
questions.solution:
To determine which metal can be extracted using carbon as a reducing agent, we need to consider the reactivity of the metals. Carbon is typically used to reduce the oxides of metals that are less reactive than carbon itself in the reactivity series. Here's a brief explanation for each metal mentioned in the options:

Option A: Zinc

Zinc is generally extracted from its ore using carbon. The chemical equation for the reduction of zinc oxide by carbon can be written as:

$$ \text{ZnO} + \text{C} \rightarrow \text{Zn} + \text{CO} $$

Here, carbon acts as a reducing agent, converting zinc oxide (ZnO) to zinc (Zn).

Option B: Silver

Silver is usually extracted by other methods such as the amalgamation process or cyanide process rather than using carbon. This is because silver is already quite unreactive and exists in nature mostly in its elemental form or in ores that do not require reduction by carbon.

Option C: Gold

Gold is even more unreactive than silver and is usually found in its native state. Therefore, it is not extracted using carbon. Instead, processes like cyanidation are employed to extract gold from its ores.

Option D: Aluminum

Aluminum is a highly reactive metal and cannot be extracted by reduction with carbon. Instead, it is extracted using electrolysis of aluminum oxide in the Hall-Héroult process.

Based on this analysis, the metal that can be extracted using carbon as a reducing agent is:

Option A: Zinc

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one among the following fertilizers is a source of nitrogen?
questions.solution:
The correct choice is Option 4: Urea.

• Urea ((NH₂)₂CO) is a highly concentrated nitrogen fertilizer (≈46% N by weight).

• Superphosphate supplies phosphorus (P₂O₅).

• Potassium sulfate supplies potassium (K₂O).

• Bone meal provides phosphorus and calcium, but only traces of nitrogen.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: The elemental composition of diamond is
questions.solution: Diamond is actually a fascinating material because it's an allotrope of carbon. This means it's made up entirely of carbon atoms, but they're arranged in a very specific, strong crystal structure. This unique arrangement of carbon atoms is what gives diamond its incredible hardness and brilliance!

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which among the following statements is not correct with respect to allotropes of Carbon?
options: Graphite is a good conductor of electricity.
Diamond is the hardest substance known.
Fullerene is one of the allotropes of carbon.
Next to diamond, graphite is the second hardest known substance.
questions.solution:
The statement which is not correct with respect to allotropes of Carbon is:

Option D: Next to diamond, graphite is the second hardest known substance.

Explanation:

Option A is correct: Graphite is a good conductor of electricity. This is because graphite has free electrons that can move through its layers.

Option B is correct: Diamond is the hardest substance known. Diamonds are renowned for their hardness.

Option C is correct: Fullerene is one of the allotropes of carbon. Fullerenes are molecules made entirely of carbon, taking the form of hollow spheres, ellipsoids, or tubes.

Option D is incorrect: Graphite is not the second hardest known substance. In fact, it is quite soft and slippery, used as a lubricant and in pencils for writing due to its layered structure.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following is the correct molecular formula of ammonium carbonate if the valency of ammonium ion is (+1) and carbonate anion is (-2)?

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following processes involves chemical reaction?
options: Storing of oxygen gas under pressure in a gas cylinder
Burning of magnesium ribbon in air
questions.solution:
The process which involves a chemical reaction is the burning of magnesium ribbon in air.

Let's examine each option to understand why:

Option A: Evaporation of petrol

Evaporation is a physical change where a liquid turns into a gas. There are no changes in the chemical composition of petrol during this process. The molecules in petrol simply gain enough energy to change their state from liquid to gas without altering their structure.

Option B: Dissolution of salt in water

When salt (sodium chloride, NaCl) dissolves in water, it dissociates into sodium ions (Na+) and chloride ions (Cl-). This is a physical process because there is no change in the chemical properties of the individual ions, and no new substances are formed.

Option C: Storing of oxygen gas under pressure in a gas cylinder

Storing oxygen gas under pressure in a gas cylinder is a physical process. The oxygen molecules (O2) remain the same, only their state of arrangement and pressure changes. There is no chemical reaction or formation of new substances involved.

Option D: Burning of magnesium ribbon in air

When magnesium ribbon burns in air, it reacts with oxygen to form magnesium oxide (MgO). This is a chemical reaction because the original substances (magnesium and oxygen) combine at the atomic level to form a new substance with different chemical properties. The chemical reaction can be represented by the following equation:

$$\text{2 Mg} + \text{O}_2 \rightarrow \text{2 MgO}$$

Hence, Option D: Burning of magnesium ribbon in air involves a chemical reaction.
